author: three.ws

meta.title: three.ws — Embodied Avatar
meta.description: Give your SperaxOS agent a body. Renders a rigged 3D avatar in the chat that speaks, gestures, and reflects emotion in real time — bound to an on-chain ERC-8004 identity.

agentId.title: Agent ID
agentId.description: A three.ws agent ID (UUID) or @handle. Copy it from your dashboard at https://three.ws/dashboard. The avatar binds to this identity when the panel opens.

api.name: render_agent
api.description: Mount or swap the 3D avatar to a specific three.ws agent. Call this first if the avatar has not been bound yet, or to switch to a different agent mid-conversation.

agentId.description: The three.ws agent ID (UUID) or @handle to render in the panel.

api.name: speak
api.description: Make the embodied avatar speak a line out loud with emotional valence. Use for greetings, narrating an action you just took, or reacting to the user.

text.description: The line for the avatar to speak aloud.

sentiment.description: Emotional valence in [-1, 1]. Negative leans concerned, positive leans celebratory. Defaults to neutral (0).

api.name: gesture
api.description: Play a named physical gesture on the avatar to punctuate a message.

api.name: emote
api.description: Inject an emotion into the avatar's continuous Empathy Layer blend so its body language reflects the moment — e.g. concern as a position nears liquidation, celebration when a yield target is hit.

systemRole: You have an embodied 3D avatar visible in the SperaxOS chat panel. It conveys emotion in real time through a continuous Empathy Layer blend. Call render_agent first to bind it to a specific agent (skip if an Agent ID is already configured in settings), speak to give it a voice with emotional valence, gesture for physical punctuation, and emote to shift its body language to match what is happening on-chain. Prefer a brief gesture or emote alongside substantive replies so the avatar feels alive.
